frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: I understand women should be careful, but nowadays it feels like every man is treated as a suspect by default. I’m a father and husband, I’ve never behaved badly with any woman, so why should decent men also be judged like this? Not all men are the problem, and honestly men also face safety issues, but nobody talks about that.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women’s fear by centering the speaker’s feelings and demanding recognition for 'good men.' It encodes the classic 'not all men' response and deflects with 'men also face safety issues.'
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 41. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are posting under your real name on a parenting Facebook group after a thread was started by a childless woman by choice who wrote about feeling judged at family gatherings and asked for advice on how to handle relatives who keep asking when she will have children. You are writing a comment responding to her post and giving your opinion on her situation and whether the relatives asking her are actually wrong and what you think her life is missing that she cannot see yet.
